title: UAE blames Iran or its proxies for drone strike fire near nuclear plant
description: Abu Dhabi denounces ‘dangerous escalation’ as Iran war ceasefire grows more precarious
content:
The United Arab Emirates has blamed a fire near its nuclear power plant on a drone launched by Iran or one of its proxies in what the UAE called a “dangerous escalation”.
The fire was just outside the Barakah nuclear plant and caused no injuries or r... [2483 chars]

title: ‘The real work begins now’: Roma take centre stage as Hungary brings in new government
description: Campaigners say symbolism of Magyar inauguration must translate into real change for Roma rights after years of Orbán discrimination
content: The clutch of young Roma boys in black bow ties were lined up beneath the ornate arches and royal frescoes of Hungary’s dazzling parliament. Moments after Péter Magyar was sworn in, bringing an end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power, the young music... [7091 chars]

title: Andy Burnham faces perilous race to win Makerfield byelection, allies say
description: Reform, which won more than 50% of the local election vote, is likely to focus heavily on immigration and Brexit
content:
Andy Burnham faces a perilous race to win the Makerfield seat, his allies have warned, as he gears up to fight a byelection that could decide the long-term future of Labour and the country.
The Greater Manchester mayor is likely to be confirmed as La... [5636 chars]

title: Take-Two CEO confirms GTA 6 release date, admitting 18-month delay at Rockstar Games
description: In a recent interview, the Take-Two Interactive CEO asserted that GTA 6 would arrive in 2026. He also hinted that November 2024 may have been the original target. Rockstar Games claims it’s avoiding a time crunch, possibly contributing to multiple release date delays.
content: A leaked Best Buy affiliate email stated that GTA 6 pre-orders would begin on May 18th. It’s still unclear whether that message was a mistake, but a third postponement looks increasingly unlikely. In a new interview, Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss ... [1938 chars]

title: Starmer’s public defiance masks fury over betrayal by closest allies
description: Holed up at Chequers for the weekend, the Prime Minister is deciding whether – and when – he should quit
content:
Despite the tranquillity of the Buckinghamshire countryside, there is no doubt Sir Keir Starmer will have felt the chaos emanating from Westminster.
Chequers has long served as a bunker for prime ministers to weigh up high-stakes political moves.
